brief_summary: Side-by-side cross-section comparing a tall sculpted spacebar (SA / MT3) and a low Cherry-profile spacebar seated over the same stabilizer wire. A coral bracket marks the generous clearance under the tall cap's inner ceiling; a bordeaux bracket marks the tight clearance under the shorter cap, labeled as new-tick risk.
data_sources: mixing-keycap-profiles.mdx § 'The three ways a mix falls apart' — 'Tall sculpted spacebars like SA and MT3 have more internal shell height above the stabilizer wire than a low Cherry-profile spacebar does ... Drop a shorter Cherry spacebar onto that same stabilizer and the wire ends can sit close enough to the cap's inner ceiling to introduce a new tick that wasn't there before the swap.'
